title: Number of European overseas colonies
subtitle: Only countries that are independent today are considered.
caption:
Number of European overseas colonies

Only countries that are independent today are considered.

This line chart tracks the number of European overseas colonies held by France, Spain, the United Kingdom, and the World total from 1462 to 2022. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 100 colonies. The orange line representing the World total shows a gradual increase starting in the 1500s, reaching approximately 45 colonies by 1800 before a sharp dip. Following this decline, the total rises steeply again after 1850, peaking just above 100 colonies around 1920, before dropping rapidly to near zero by the late 20th century. The United Kingdom, shown in purple, maintains low numbers until the mid-19th century, then climbs to a peak between 50 and 60 colonies around 1920, mirroring the global trend before falling. France, depicted in pink, remains near the bottom of the chart for most of the timeline, rising to a peak of roughly 25 to 30 colonies in the early 1900s before declining. Spain, represented by the green line, holds a steady count of roughly 20 colonies from the 1600s until 1800, after which the line drops significantly and remains low. By the end of the timeline in 2022, all lines converge at the bottom of the chart, indicating zero colonies for the tracked entities.
